description:
Port of fz-aiz.6 from the unmerged `fz-aiz/any-key-floor` branch.
Opt-in diagnostic that flags callsites dispatching to the generic
any-key spec when narrower specs exist for the same callee.

## Verified state on main

- Diagnostic framework exists at ir_typer.rs:2520
  (`collect_diagnostics(module, types)` walks `types.specs` by FnId,
  emits per-spec unreachable-arm warnings). Extensible.
- `SpecRegistry::iter()` yields `(SpecId, FnId, key)` tuples
  (existing use at ir_codegen.rs:6338).
- Any-key identification is trivial: the spec whose key equals
  `vec![Descr::any(); arity]`.
- No `FZ_WSPEC_QUALITY` env var or sibling diagnostic flag found
  in codebase — clean addition.

## What lands

Sibling walker to `collect_diagnostics`. For each reachable spec
body:
- At each direct `Term::Call` / `Term::TailCall`, replicate the
  spec_registry resolve logic against the caller's per-spec
  `FnTypes`.
- Warn when the resolved spec's key equals `[any; arity]` AND ≥1
  narrower spec is registered for the same FnId.
- Diagnostic content: the inferred per-arg Descrs at this site, the
  list of narrower specs registered for the callee, a help hint
  about widening one of the narrow specs or adding an `@spec` to
  the caller.

Excluded: CallClosure / TailCallClosure / Receive sites — opaque
dispatch is correct by design at those seams.

Gating: `FZ_WSPEC_QUALITY=1` env var. Off by default; CI can
enable on curated fixtures to catch specialization-quality
regressions.

## Diagnostic shape (target output)

```
warning[type/spec-quality]: call to `fib` from `fib` dispatches to the any-key (generic) spec
  --> <span>
   = inferred args here: [int | float, any, int | float]
   = note: 8 narrower specs of `fib` registered for other call sites:
      [0, 0, 1]
      [1, 0, 1]
      [10, 0, 1]
      [20, 0, 1]
      [int | float, 1, int]
      [int | float, int, int]
      [int, 1, int]
      [int, int, int]
   = help: the call runs correctly through the generic body, but pays
     the runtime-dispatch cost the narrower specializations avoid.
     either widen one of the narrow specs to cover this site, or add
     a `@spec` annotation to the caller to force narrowing here.
```

## Dependency

Soft depends on fz-rcp.3. Without `ensure_any_keys`, direct-only fns
don't have any-keys, so the diagnostic produces no signal for the
most common shape (any-key is what we'd warn against, and it's only
present for opaque-dispatch fns today). Land after fz-rcp.3 for the
diagnostic to be useful.

Independent of fz-rcp.4 / fz-rcp.5 — reads the registry directly,
not callsite_outcomes.

## Acceptance

- Enabling `FZ_WSPEC_QUALITY=1` on fib_tailrec produces the
  expected diagnostic shape above.
- No effect when disabled (default off).
- Unit test for diagnostic content shape.
- 618 unit + 166 fixture-matrix tests green (with flag disabled).
title: fz-rcp.6: --Wspec-quality diagnostic — opt-in specialization-quality warning
